name: Sotillo de la Ribera and Mirador del
name_original: Sotillo de la Ribera y Mirador del Castillo — circular desde Oquillas
description: This moderate 56.9-mile road cycling route from Oquillas explores the Ribera del Duero, featuring vineyards and the Mirador del Castillo.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Oquillas are situated in the rural Burgos province of Spain. The region features a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural areas. Elevations vary, offering routes with significant climbs and descents. This terrain provides diverse conditions for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Oquillas The most popular road cycling route is Sotillo de la Ribera and Mirador del, a 56.9 miles (91.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route features notable elevation changes and scenic viewpoints. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Loop, a moderate 24.2 miles (39.0 km) path. This route traverses the Ribera del Duero wine region and the Esgueva Valley, offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Church of Espinosa de Cervera – Caleruega loop from Valdurros, a 31.3 miles (50.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Oquillas is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and easy routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Oquillas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Oquillas?
A: The Oquillas region, located in the rural Burgos province of Spain, is characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural areas. You'll encounter routes with significant climbs and descents, offering diverse conditions for road cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Oquillas?
A: Yes, the area offers options for various ability levels. There are easy routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana del Pidio loop from Shrine of San Pedro Regalado&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.1 miles (16.3 km) with moderate elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31473373" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintana del Pidio loop from Quintana del Pidio&lt;/a&gt;, a similar 10.3 miles (16.5 km) route.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the Oquillas area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055794028/sotillo-de-la-ribera-and-mirador-del" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo de la Ribera and Mirador del&lt;/a&gt; route is a substantial 56.9 miles (91.5 km) trail, typically taking over 4 hours to complete with notable elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Oquillas?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided, the easier routes with shorter distances and less intense elevation, like the loops around Quintana del Pidio, could be suitable for families depending on their experience level. These routes traverse rural landscapes with generally lower traffic.

Q: What kind of landmarks or points of interest can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle through the Oquillas region, you can explore various points of interest. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6086751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Cipriano&lt;/a&gt;, and charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6087942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañes de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5569849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5529442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cilleruelo de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gumiel de Izán&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Oquillas?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the Oquillas area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308702665/ribera-del-duero-and-esgueva-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39836399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Espinosa de Cervera – Caleruega loop from Valdurros&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, allowing you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Oquillas?
A: Given the region's agricultural and rural landscape, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for road cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder conditions, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: How many road cycling routes are featured in this guide for Oquillas?
A: This guide highlights 5 distinct road cycling routes around Oquillas, ranging in difficulty from easy to moderate, and showcasing the best of the region's varied terrain.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop along the road cycling routes in Oquillas?
A: The routes often pass through or near small villages and settlements like Sotillo de la Ribera and Gumiel de Izán. These locations typically offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, or small shops for refreshments and breaks, especially in the Ribera del Duero wine region.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of the road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking information for each route start is not detailed, as the routes often begin in or near villages, you can typically find public parking options within these settlements. It's advisable to check maps or local information for specific parking facilities at your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling in the Oquillas region?
A: Road cycling in Oquillas is characterized by rolling hills and varied elevations. The routes in this guide are primarily rated as moderate, with some easier options available. This means you can expect a good workout with climbs and descents, but nothing overly extreme for experienced road cyclists.
tips: In this town, which is close to the A-1 road, we find the town of Bahabón de Esgueva. There is Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de la Asunción where there is usually a large stork's nest at the top of the bell tower. You can still see houses made of bitumen, the mud that was once used as a brick.
From the top of the viewpoint you can see an impressive panorama of the town of Valdeande. In addition, at its feet, you can see some ancient undergrounds that had been active years ago.

One of the most beautiful towns in the south of the province of Burgos, as there is a spectacular church called Iglesia de Santa Águeda. Along with its bell tower, there is a watchtower, narrow in an ecclesiastical construction.
On the side of the road there is this spring that can give us water on the hottest days of these Burgos lands.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 91.5 kilometers long and is estimated to take about 4 hours and 5 minutes to complete on a road bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sotillo de la Ribera y Mirador del Castillo route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for road bikes. However, some segments might be unpaved and more challenging, potentially requiring cyclists to push their bikes if their fitness level is not very good.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is graded as moderate and requires a good level of fitness due to its length (91.5 km) and elevation gain (736 meters). It's best suited for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque scenery as the route traverses the Ribera del Duero, a region renowned for its winemaking. Cyclists will enjoy peaceful roads winding through vineyards, charming Castilian towns, and natural beauty with hills and riverside forests. The name "Mirador del Castillo" also suggests a viewpoint with castle vistas.

faqs.question: What are some notable points of interest on this route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll pass through charming settlements like Bahabón de Esgueva and Sotillo de la Ribera. You can also visit the Castle Viewpoint and the natural monument Fuente del Tío Sixto.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for Oquillas are not provided, as a circular route, you can typically find parking within the village of Oquillas or a nearby town to begin your ride. Look for public parking areas or consult local maps upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Ribera del Duero region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant with either blooming vineyards or harvest colors. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter weather.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, dogs are generally not permitted to accompany cyclists on the road for safety reasons. If you plan to stop in towns, ensure your dog is leashed and follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route through the Ribera del Duero region.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the Sendero de las Ermitas, Enebral, Sendero del Gromejón, and Ruta de Los Molinos, though it primarily follows its own course.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your road bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Consider carrying a small first-aid kit as well.
